column1: # Latin transliteration rules over Arabic Unicode tokens (QAC forms).
# The engine (arabiyya/translit_ar.py) normalises tokens first — mater
# folding, diphthongs, gemination, tanwin — and looks the results up here.
# Every value is editable; the engine adds no mappings of its own.
# Known v0 limits, deliberate and flagged:
#  * Article-lam assimilation IS applied (an orthographic reading rule:
#    bare lam after wasla before a geminate is the absorbed article), so
#    ٱلرَّحْمَٰن renders arraḥmān; hyphenation (ar-raḥmān) is not attempted.
#  * Alef wasla is drafted as plain "a" (its standalone-headword reading);
#  * Bare teh marbuta (pausal) drafts as "h"; with a vowel it is "t".
#  * Qur'anic annotation signs (small letters, pause marks) are dropped
#    with a flag — they are recitation apparatus, not lexical content.

column3: wasla: no consonant of its own; the engine supplies vowel a — standalone reading, elision not modelled
consonantal alef only; matres are folded by the engine
consonantal waw; ū matres folded by the engine
consonantal use only; ā matres folded by the engine
consonantal yeh; ī matres folded by the engine
